task: Write a Python function second_largest(nums) returning the second largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than two distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function does not handle the edge case of an empty list.
trace: First, I sorted the list in reverse order to ensure the largest and second largest numbers are at the end of the list. Then, I checked if there were less than two distinct values in the list, and returned None if that was the case. Otherwise, I returned the second element (the second largest number) from the sorted list. The edge case I handled is when the list has fewer than two unique elements.
